title: angelica anomala var. sachalinensis
intro: Angelica anomala var. sachalinensis, also known as Angelica or Sachalin Angelica, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to the island of Sakhalin in Russia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and aiding in digestion, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a dried root or in capsule form.
